reg-032.title_en: The Hague: Fossil-Fuel OOH Advertising Ban Survives Interim Legal Challenge — Court Lets Ban Stand, April 2025
reg-032.zusammenfassung: The Hague introduced the world's first APV-based ban on fossil outdoor advertising. Since 1 January 2025, the ban applies to advertising in public space for fossil fuels, air holidays, airline tickets, grey electricity contracts, gas contracts, cruises, and cars with fossil-fuel or hybrid engines. In April 2025, Rechtbank Den Haag rejected interim claims by ANVR, TUI, D-Reizen, and Prijsvrij and let the ban stand.
reg-032.kernbefund: The court held that the municipality had sufficiently substantiated that the advertising ban can contribute to protecting health and reducing negative climate impacts. It also held that, in these interim proceedings, the ban was not evidently unlawful or incompatible with Dutch constitutional or EU rules. Important caveat: this is a preliminary interim ruling, not a binding EU-wide precedent.
reg-032.relevanz: The decision is a strong signal for municipal advertising regulation: a locally grounded fossil-fuel OOH ban can survive court scrutiny when the municipality clearly substantiates public health, climate, and environmental objectives. For OOH/DOOH operators this increases legal and contract risk in cities considering similar bans, but it does not replace jurisdiction-specific legal assessment in other EU member states.

reg-033.title_en: Spanish Competition Authority CNMC Blocks JCDecaux Acquisition of Clear Channel Spain — Market Power Too High (October 2024)
reg-033.zusammenfassung: In October 2024, the Spanish competition authority CNMC prohibited the planned acquisition of Clear Channel's Spanish operations by JCDecaux. The regulators found that the merger would create a dominant market position that permanently eliminates competition in the Spanish OOH market.
reg-033.kernbefund: The CNMC analysis concluded that following a successful acquisition JCDecaux would control over 70% of premium OOH surfaces in major Spanish cities. The authority found no adequate remedies to compensate for the competitive distortions. The proceedings demonstrate that EU competition authorities are actively combating structural market concentration in the OOH sector.
reg-033.relevanz: The decision confirms what the EU Commission had already established in earlier cases (JCDecaux Brussels, reg-023): the OOH sector is prone to oligopolistic structures that require state intervention. Cities and municipalities that enter into contracts with OOH corporations do so within a structurally imbalanced power dynamic.

reg-034.title_en: UN Special Rapporteur Elisa Morgera calls for fossil advertising bans and criminalization of fossil disinformation (2025)
reg-034.zusammenfassung: UN Special Rapporteur on human rights and climate change Elisa Morgera called in report A/HRC/59/42 of 15 May 2025 for far-reaching action against fossil-fuel disinformation. The recommendations include banning fossil-fuel advertising, promotion and sponsorship, and criminalising misinformation and greenwashing by the fossil-fuel industry.
reg-034.kernbefund: Morgera recommends that states ban fossil-fuel advertising, promotion and sponsorship, including cross-border advertising. She also recommends that media and advertising firms can be criminalised when they amplify disinformation or misinformation by fossil-fuel companies. The report is not OOH-specific, but outdoor advertising is part of the fossil-fuel advertising and promotion system it targets.
reg-034.relevanz: For OOH/DOOH, the report is a strong policy signal: fossil outdoor advertising is framed not merely as consumer or climate communication, but as part of a human-rights-relevant disinformation system. Network operators, agencies and media owners should treat fossil-fuel campaigns as governance, reputation and future regulatory risk.

psych-010.title_en: 95-Study Systematic Review: Advertising Causally Produces Body Image Harm in Adolescents — Strongest Evidence to Date (Frontiers 2025)
psych-010.zusammenfassung: A meta-analysis of 95 experimental studies published in Frontiers in Psychology (2025) concludes that advertising featuring idealised body images causally produces body dissatisfaction, self-esteem problems, and disordered eating behaviour in adolescents and young adults. It is the most comprehensive synthesis of causal evidence on this topic to date.
psych-010.kernbefund: The meta-analysis finds significant, consistent effects across 95 experiments: exposure to idealised body images in advertising increases body dissatisfaction (effect size d=0.58), depressive mood (d=0.41), and disordered eating behaviour (d=0.37). The effects hold across all genders and are particularly strong in early teenagers.
psych-010.relevanz: Outdoor advertising is the most public of all advertising channels — children and adolescents cannot avoid it. The systematic evidence of this meta-analysis directly supports legislative bans on idealising body images in OOH campaigns. The psychological harm caused by outdoor advertising is therefore no longer scientifically deniable.

pol-016.title_en: Ströer Exceeds €2 Billion Annual Revenue for the First Time in 2024 — Controls 80% of the German DOOH Market
pol-016.zusammenfassung: Ströer SE & Co. KGaA reported record revenues of over €2 billion for the 2024 financial year and states it controls approximately 80% of DOOH advertising surfaces in Germany. The company markets more than 500,000 advertising structures in public space — giving a single corporation monopoly control over the visual landscape of German city centres.
pol-016.kernbefund: Market concentration at Ströer has grown continuously since 2010. Ströer holds exclusive concession contracts with cities, municipalities, and Deutsche Bahn with terms of 10–25 years. At the same time, the corporation is investing heavily in DOOH conversions that inherit existing permits without new approval procedures. The €2B revenue figure is more than double the total public-sector expenditure on urban green space in Germany.
pol-016.relevanz: When a single publicly listed company controls 80% of the national DOOH market while using public space under conditions that bind cities for decades, a structural dependency arises that makes democratic regulation more difficult. Ströer's lobbying power is proportional to its market power.

priv-005.title_en: EU AI Act: Biometric Categorisation of Persons in Public Space Prohibited — DOOH Audience Measurement Directly Affected
priv-005.zusammenfassung: The EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689), in force since August 2024, classifies biometric categorisation systems in public space as high-risk. Systems that infer emotion, age, gender, or ethnicity from camera footage are subject to strict requirements or are prohibited. DOOH audience measurement technology (Anonymized Video Analytics) falls directly within this regulatory scope.
priv-005.kernbefund: Article 5 of the AI Act prohibits, with effect from February 2025, the real-time analysis of biometric characteristics for the purpose of categorising persons in publicly accessible spaces without explicit authorisation. Most DOOH audience measurement systems (JCDecaux Smart, Clear Channel Live, Ströer Data Analytics) use precisely these technologies. The Commerzbank cooperation with Ströer for target-group analysis would no longer be legal under Art. 5 of the AI Act.
priv-005.relevanz: DOOH screens equipped with cameras for audience analysis become a heavily regulated high-risk area under the EU AI Act. The data collection promise that enables programmatic DOOH targeting is highly legally uncertain under EU law. Advertisers relying on audience data from DOOH network operators expose themselves to liability risks.
